name: American Lasagna (Usda)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap. Allow enough extra wrap to fold over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans for food to be served without freezing.
2. Crumble the beef and cook until lightly browned. Stir in onion and cook until onion is tender. Drain off fat.
3. Stir in seasonings, tomato paste, tomato sauce, and water. Simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. In another bowl, blend eggs with cottage cheese.
5. In each pan, spread a layer of meat mixture (about 3/4 cup).
6. Add a layer of noodles (2 1/4), meat mixture (about 3/4 cup), cottage cheese mixture (about 3/4 cup), processed cheese (1/3 cup), and Parmesan cheese (1 1/2 tablespoons).
7. Repeat layers until all ingredients are used. Pack the food tightly to avoid air pockets.
To Serve Without Freezing:
Preheat oven to 400° F. Bake for 30 minutes or until sauce bubbles at edges.
To Freeze:
Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature. Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over top of food. Put edges of wrap together and fold several times so paper lies directly on top of food. Fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape. Label with name of food, date of freezing, and last date the food should be used for best eating quality (about 6 months). Freeze for 10-12 hours before removing packages from the pans.
To Heat Frozen Food:
Preheat oven to 400° F. Remove freezer wrap. Place food in baking pan. Bake for 1 1/4 hours or until sauce bubbles at edges and center is hot.
Variation: Italian Lasagna - Use ricotta cheese in place of cottage cheese and mozzarella cheese in place of processed cheddar cheese.
